analysis.problem: Erosion of trust in political leadership and institutions among key demographic groups
analysis.root_cause: Polarization, misinformation, and declining public confidence in the ability of leaders to address critical issues like climate change, public health, and social equity
leverage_points: Media and information ecosystems that amplify polarization and misinformation
Political leaders and their ability to communicate transparently and act on public concerns

proposed_actions.action: Implement media literacy programs in schools and public institutions
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase public understanding of misinformation and critical thinking skills by 30% within 5 years

proposed_actions.action: Create independent oversight bodies to monitor political communication and transparency
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce instances of misleading public statements by political leaders by 40% within 3 years

evidence: Two-thirds of Latino Trump voters (66%) approve of his job performance, down 27 percentage points since the start of his second term
A majority of Americans (44%) say the country’s best years are behind us, and are more pessimistic about the future than optimistic
analysis.generalization: This issue connects to the broader domain of social cohesion and trust in institutions, which are foundational for sustainable civilization and peace
analysis.summary: Public trust in political leadership is eroding, driven by polarization and misinformation, threatening social cohesion and long-term stability.

raw_items.title: Trump’s approval rating hits second-term low among his Latino voters, though many still approve
raw_items.summary: About two-thirds of Latino Trump voters (66%) approve of his job performance. That’s down 27 percentage points since the start of his second term.

raw_items.title: A majority of Americans say the country’s best years are behind us
raw_items.summary: Americans are also much more pessimistic (44%) than optimistic (28%) when asked to think about what things will be like in the U.S. 50 years from now.

raw_items.title: What Americans think it takes to be a good news consumer
raw_items.summary: One of the most common things Americans associate with being a good news consumer is a sense of skepticism or discernment.

raw_items.title: How Americans Feel About Religion’s Influence in Government and Public Life
raw_items.summary: A growing share of U.S. adults say religion is gaining influence, but most still want churches to stay out of politics, a 2026 survey finds.

raw_items.title: Trump warns 'clock is ticking' for Iran as peace progress stalls
raw_items.summary: Iranian media reports the US has not made concrete concessions in response to Tehran's latest proposals.

raw_items.title: Driver of crashed train tested positive for drugs, Thai police say
raw_items.summary: The freight train collided with a bus in Bangkok, killing eight people and injuring dozens of others.

raw_items.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo an international emergency
raw_items.summary: The agency added that the outbreak, with around 246 cases and 80 deaths, does not meet the criteria of pandemic emergency.

raw_items.title: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?
raw_items.summary: An outbreak in DR Congo has been declared a health emergency, with almost 250 suspected cases reported.
